description:
A security analyst needs to audit the contents of an encrypted volume recovered from a departing employee's workstation. Mount the VeraCrypt volume in read-only mode to preserve evidence integrity, then create a file inventory report.

Specifically:
1. Mount the encrypted volume at /home/ga/Volumes/data_volume.hc to the mount point /home/ga/MountPoints/slot1. The volume MUST be mounted read-only (write-protected). Password: MountMe2024, PIM: 0 (default), no keyfiles, no hidden volume protection.
2. List the contents of the mounted volume (filenames, sizes, and modification dates).
3. Save this listing as a plain text file to /home/ga/Documents/volume_inventory.txt. Each file in the volume root should appear in the inventory.
4. Leave the volume mounted read-only when finished — do NOT dismount it.

The inventory file should contain real listing output (not just filenames), such as output from 'ls -la' or similar.
